name: Running
tagline: Speed is the name of the game!
rules: All participants must report 15 minutes before their event.
Standard athletics track rules apply. False starts result in disqualification on the second offence.
Relay baton must be passed within the exchange zone. Dropping the baton is allowed — you may pick it up.
Lane assignment is done by draw. No lane switching after the start.
Results are final as declared by the timekeeper.
Minimum 2 participants from each team must compete in each individual event.
Relay team composition must be declared 30 minutes before the event.

name: Badminton
rules: BWF rules apply. Best of 3 games to 21 points with a 2-point lead. Cap at 30-29.
Service must be below the waist. Foot fault calls are final.
Players must use provided shuttlecocks only. Rackets are personal.
A 5-minute warm-up is allowed before matches begin.
If a player is not present within 5 minutes of being called, they forfeit the match.
Coaching from the sidelines is not allowed during play.
Each team must field at least 1 player per event category.

name: Chess
rules: FIDE rules apply. Touch move is strictly enforced — if you touch a piece you must move it.
Time control: 15 minutes per player per game. No increment.
Clock must be pressed with the same hand used to move the piece.
Illegal moves result in a 2-minute time penalty; second illegal move is a forfeit.
No mobile phones or electronic devices allowed at the board.
Draws by agreement can only be offered after move 20.
Pairings are made using Swiss system — you will not face the same opponent twice.
In case of a score tie, tiebreakers are: Buchholz score, then Sonneborn-Berger.

name: Carrom
rules: ICF (International Carrom Federation) rules apply.
Each board game is played to 25 points or 8 boards, whichever comes first.
Players must strike from within their baseline. Overhanging coins must be replaced.
The queen must be covered (pocketed with a coin in the same strike) to score it.
If the striker falls into a pocket, the opponent receives 1 coin from the board as penalty.
Doubles partners sit opposite each other. No switching positions mid-game.
Team composition for doubles must be declared before the round robin draw.
Two teams from each group stage advance to the knockout rounds.

name: Dream11 – IPL Final
tagline: Build your squad. Own the game!
rules: Each team must submit one fantasy squad of exactly 11 players from the IPL Final match.
Squad must include: 1 WK, 3-5 Batters, 1-3 All-Rounders, 3-5 Bowlers.
Maximum 7 players from any single IPL team in your fantasy squad.
Designate 1 Captain (2× points) and 1 Vice-Captain (1.5× points) from your squad.
Squads must be submitted at least 30 minutes before the match toss.
Squads cannot be changed after the submission deadline.
Points are calculated based on official Dream11 scoring system (reference values in points table above).
The team with the highest total fantasy points wins the event.
In case of a tie, the team with the higher captain's points wins.
Winning team earns 40 team points; Runner-up earns 25; 3rd place earns 15.
